hypothesis: If crystal material property prediction integrates complementary information from graph structural, natural language textual, and X-ray diffraction spectral modalities through cross-modal predictive learning in a shared latent space, then the resulting multimodal model will achieve lower mean absolute error on benchmark crystal property prediction tasks than unimodal graph-based models (such as CGCNN and ALIGNN), because each modality captures distinct physicochemical features that collectively provide a richer representation of material structure than any single modality alone.
